name: Garrett v. United States
summary: Held that a continuing criminal enterprise offense and a predicate drug importation offense were not the same for double jeopardy purposes because the CCE statute was designed to reach distinct, ongoing criminal conduct.
significance: Cited as an example where cumulative punishment was authorized without explicit 'in addition to' language, based on statutory structure and purpose — a continuing criminal enterprise spanning years was held to be a different offense from a single predicate drug importation.
